name: Varnek Karn, the Death's Negotiator
description:
Varnek Karn, the Death's Negotiator

*{Lore Varnek Karn dwells in the gray between life and death, where the newly departed linger with unfinished business and elder shades guard secrets dearer than gold. He is the broker who speaks across the veil, the mediator of disputes between living and dead, and the archivist of every promise sworn beyond the grave. His servants practice post-mortem diplomacy: they negotiate, consult, and contract with spirits as equals --- mindful that the dead are patient and rarely generous.

Yet Varnek Karn is also the patron of those who raise the dead, not through force but through bargain. The dead may be persuaded to walk again, to fight for a season, or to guard a threshold, but every such compact is a debt. The dead demand their due: a memory, a future, a name, or simply the promise that the living will remember them. Those who serve Varnek Karn learn that raising a spirit is never a command; it is a negotiation, and the spirit always has a price.

Among the peoples of Fate's Edge, Varnek Karn is the patron of those who treat with the departed respectfully. In Aeler, he is the Breath-Counter, who audits the spiritual ledgers of the Deep Vaults and ensures that the dead are not forgotten. In Ykrul, he is the Bone-Speaker, whose drum calls ancestors to council before any major migration. In Vhasia, he is the Silent Notary, who witnesses death-bed testaments and ensures their terms are honored. In the Mistlands, he is the Bell-Shrouded, whose unknotted rope holds the fog at bay. Wherever the living and the dead must negotiate, Varnek Karn's servants are there --- not to command, but to listen.

quote "The dead have all the time in the world to consider their offers, and no need to rush to agreement. They know what the living forgot, and remember every slight with perfect clarity. In Varnek's halls, both parties pay a price --- but the currency is never what you expect." quote

*{Domain Focus itemize Post-Mortem Diplomacy: parleys with ghosts and recent dead Ancestral Wisdom: counsel from forebears and keeping of funerary law Unfinished Business: resolving the concerns that bind spirits Death-Bound Contracts: obligations that outlive the flesh The Unquiet Host: raising temporary armies of the dead through pact itemize

*{Thiasos and Codex (Runekeeper Options)

A Runekeeper sworn to Varnek Karn does not bind a living familiar. Their Thiasos is a remnant of the honored dead: a lead seal that has witnessed a funeral, a knucklebone from an ancestor who died content, or a small iron bell that rings only when a spirit is willing to speak. This object requires no food, but it must be placed on a grave or threshold at dusk each day. If neglected for a week, the Thiasos becomes Compromised (it emits a faint keening that imposes --1 die on all social rolls with the living).

The Codex of Varnek Karn is never a living book. It may be a slate carved with names that fade and reappear, a bundle of burial tablets, or the inside of a lead-lined casket. Because the dead are not bound by paper, the Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week recording a new death or a settled dispute, or the Rites stored there become illegible (treat as Compromised until restored).

*{Patron's Gift (Imbuement)

Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of Varnek Karn may touch a held item (weapon, tool, or funerary token) and whisper a death-bargain. For the rest of the scene, the item grants +1 die to Lore or Investigation rolls involving death, ancestry, or inheritance, and the bearer may ask one question of a corpse less than a day dead (it answers with a single word or a gesture). After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to Varnek Karn.
summary: Varnek Karn, the Death's Negotiator *{Lore Varnek Karn dwells in the gray between life and death, where the newly departed linger with unfinished business and elder shades guard secrets dearer than gold.

rites.name: Rite of the Crossing Fee
rites.description: (Basic, 4 XP) Scene; Near; No. Tags: , Materials: A coin for the tongue of the deceased, or a lock of hair from the bereaved. Effect: Open speech with a specific dead individual. Gain +1 die to Investigation or Lore when seeking knowledge they once held. Push It: The spirit offers a deeper fragment of memory, but demands a future favor in return. Mark 1 SB (Hearts). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Open speech with a specific dead individual. Gain +1 die to Investigation or Lore when seeking knowledge they once held. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Ancestral Consultation
rites.description: (Basic, 5 XP) Scene; Self; Yes. Tags: , , Materials: A personal token of the departed, or their favored food or drink. Effect: Ask one question of an ancestral spirit regarding family history, hidden legacies, or inherited debts. Push It: The spirit grants detailed, prophetic guidance but exacts a small favor; mark 1 SB (Hearts). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Ask one question of an ancestral spirit regarding family history, hidden legacies, or inherited debts. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Mediated Pact
rites.description: (Advanced, 8 XP) Scene; Near; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: Two copies of a written accord (one buried with the dead, one kept by the living), or a circle of salt mixed with grave-dust. Effect: Choose one: itemize Death-Bound Pact: Bind a compact between a living signatory and a willing spirit. Both gain +1 die when fulfilling the terms. Breach angers the spirit and Varnek both. Spirit Medium: Serve as a conduit for several spirits to speak. Gain +2 dice to mediate disputes between living and dead and to facilitate group negotiation. itemize Push It: If you chose Pact, the pact enforces itself: a breach triggers supernatural reprisal; mark +1 Obligation. If you chose Medium, a potent spirit rides your voice to speak directly; mark Exposure +1. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Choose one: itemize Death-Bound Pact: Bind a compact between a living signatory and a willing spirit. Both gain +1 die when fulfilling the terms. Breach angers the spirit and Varnek both. Spirit Medium: Serve as a conduit for several spirits to speak. Gain +2 dice to mediate disputes between living and dead and to facilitate group negotiation. itemize

rites.name: Rite of the Unquiet Host
rites.description: (Advanced, 10 XP) Scene; Zone; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: A battlefield relic (a broken sword, a skull, a scrap of uniform) and a handful of grave-earth. Effect: You negotiate with the lingering dead of a place. Summon a Temporary Host -- a spectral warband treated as a single Cap 3 Follower with the following traits: itemize Assist: May grant +3 dice to a single combat or intimidation roll, or provide a free Setup action (e.g., "they distract the guards"). Independent Action: Once per scene, the Host may take one action from the Follower list (e.g., Scout & Signal, Distract & Draw) at the cost of marking Exposure +1. Weakness: The Host is vulnerable to salt, bells, and consecrated ground. Any attack using such materials ignores their spectral nature. itemize The Host lasts for the remainder of the current scene, or until dismissed. When it departs, the dead demand their due: you must either mark +2 Obligation or perform a Memorial Rite before the next dawn (a downtime action, no roll). If you fail to do so, the Host returns unbidden at the worst moment, acting as a hostile hazard (GM's discretion). Push It: Extend the Host's duration to the entire session. The debt is doubled: mark +4 Obligation or perform a memorial rite and sacrifice a personal memory (erase a minor fact or a Bond for one session). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.action: Once per scene, the Host may take one action from the Follower list (e.g., Scout \& Signal, Distract \& Draw) at the cost of marking Exposure +1.
rites.effect: You negotiate with the lingering dead of a place. Summon a Temporary Host -- a spectral warband treated as a single Cap 3 Follower with the following traits: itemize Assist: May grant +3 dice to a single combat or intimidation roll, or provide a free Setup action (e.g., ``they distract the guards''). Independent Action: Once per scene, the Host may take one action from the Follower list (e.g., Scout \& Signal, Distract \& Draw) at the cost of marking Exposure +1. Weakness: The Host is vulnerable to salt, bells, and consecrated ground. Any attack using such materials ignores their spectral nature. itemize The Host lasts for the remainder of the current scene, or until dismissed. When it departs, the dead demand their due: you must either mark +2 Obligation or perform a Memorial Rite before the next dawn (a downtime action, no roll). If you fail to do so, the Host returns unbidden at the worst moment, acting as a hostile hazard (GM's discretion). \\

rites.name: Rite of the Final Testament
rites.description: (Master, 13 XP) Extended; Touch; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: A dying person's last words recorded in their blood, witnessed by you. Effect: Seal the last request with weight beyond life. Targets must pass Resolve (DV 4) to ignore it. Push It: The testament becomes a geas: only fulfillment --- or Varnek's wrath --- can break it; mark +2 Obligation. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +4) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Permanent; Obligation timer: 7 segments.
rites.effect: Seal the last request with weight beyond life. Targets must pass Resolve (DV 4) to ignore it. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Eternal Archive
rites.description: (Master, 14 XP) Extended; Zone; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: A library mortared with tomb-stone, ink from cremation ash. Effect: Found a repository where the dead deposit knowledge. Gain a permanent +1 die to Lore for historical or genealogical research made within. Spirits periodically contribute testimonies. Push It: The archive awakens and begins to demand curatorship and payment; mark Harm 1 (Stress). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Permanent; Obligation timer: 8 segments.
rites.effect: Found a repository where the dead deposit knowledge. Gain a permanent +1 die to Lore for historical or genealogical research made within. Spirits periodically contribute testimonies. \\
